title: Stewed tomatoes
cScaleSummary: C0 because the only cholesterol contributor is the very small amount of fish sauce, which remains effectively negligible when divided across 4 servings. The tomatoes, onion, olive oil, stock, soy sauce, salt, and black pepper do not materially contribute cholesterol.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL-Active because the dish is vegetable-forward, uses modest olive oil, and builds flavor through slow onion cooking, tomato structure, restrained stock use, and umami rather than saturated fat. Its overall structure supports LDL-friendly eating.
intro: Stewed tomatoes like this show up all over Midwestern and Mid-Atlantic tables, especially next to baked macaroni and cheese. The goal is contrast. Soft, rich pasta against bright, savory tomato with just enough body to cling. This version keeps the traditional structure, relying on high-quality whole tomatoes, controlled onion sweetness, and a restrained finish so the dish stays clean and defined.

stepGroups.title: Build the Onion Base
items: Start with a cold sauce pan.. Add the olive oil, onion, and salt. Bring up to medium heat and cook slowly, stirring frequently, until the onion is fully softened, pale golden, and completely collapsed with no browned edges, 12 to 15 minutes. The onion should smell sweet and mellow, not toasted.

stepGroups.title: Add and Structure the Tomatoes
items: Add the whole peeled tomatoes with their juice. Using your hand, crush 1 tomato firmly through your fist into the pot to seed the base with body.
Bring to a very low simmer and allow the remaining tomatoes to heat and relax for about 5 minutes.
Add the stock, soy sauce, fish sauce, salt, and black pepper. Stir gently to combine.

stepGroups.title: Controlled Simmer
items: Bring to a very low simmer. The surface should show only occasional small bubbles. Maintain this for 60 minutes, stirring gently every 10 minutes to prevent sticking. Do not agitate the tomatoes.
The tomatoes should soften and slump while holding shape.

stepGroups.title: Finish
items: Using a slotted spoon, transfer the tomato flesh and onions to a serving dish. Ladle a small amount of the stewing liquid over the top to moisten and gloss the tomatoes without flooding the dish. Do not use all of the liquid.
